user_outcome: A pull request that targets `main` without bumping the version is blocked by a fail-closed CI gate, so a release can never ship without the reported version advancing.
rationale: Depends on S01: the gate compares internal/version/version.txt (the single source S01 establishes) between the PR head and the base (main). The check is fail-closed — an unchanged or non-incremented version is a failure, not a pass. Implemented as an isolated GitHub Actions workflow (A-02) rather than a job appended to ci.yml, for clarity. Comparison is strictly-greater semver (A-01), so a no-op edit does not satisfy it.

effort_complexity.rationale: One workflow file + a small compare step/script. No production code. The only subtlety is the semver compare and reading the base-ref file.

acceptance_criteria.text: When a pull request targets `main`, the version-bump workflow SHALL read internal/version/version.txt on the PR head and on the base (main) and SHALL fail the check unless the head version is a strictly-greater semver than the base version.
If internal/version/version.txt is unchanged, malformed, or not strictly greater than the base, then the gate SHALL exit non-zero (fail closed) with a message naming the base and head versions.

The gate SHALL run only for PRs whose base is `main` (the protected branch), so feature/release-branch PRs are not blocked; this SHALL be expressed in the workflow trigger (on: pull_request: branches: [main]).

When scripts/check-version-bump.sh is run locally with BASE and HEAD version arguments, it SHALL exit 0 only if HEAD > BASE (semver) and non-zero otherwise — so the logic is testable without GitHub. Reachability artefact: a documented local invocation showing a pass (0.1.0 -> 0.1.1) and a fail (0.1.0 -> 0.1.0).
